name: Roasted Garlic
recipeInstructions:
1 Preheat oven to 375 degrees F.
2 Remove the papery outer covering of whole garlic head. Do not separate or peel the cloves.
3 Place as many whole heads of garlic on a large square of heavy-duty aluminum foil as there are people to be served. Fold up foil so that the cloves are completely wrapped.
4 Bake in the preheated oven for 1 hour and 15 minutes.
5 Serve each diner a head of garlic and some bread and butter. Separate cloves.
6 Hold a clove over a piece of buttered bread and squeeze. The garlic puree will pop out. Spread and eat.
Garlic Puree:
1 Let roasted heads of garlic cool, unwrapped for at least 5 minutes.
2 Gently separate the cloves and squeeze each one over a fine-mesh sieve so that the softened garlic pops into the sieve.
3 With a wooden spatula or wooden spoon, rub garlic through the sieve into a small container or bowl.
4 Cover tightly with plastic wrap and refrigerate the puree if needed.
Quick Puree:
1 If in a hurry, simply squeeze the cloves, one by one, over a bowl.
2 When all have been squeezed, use a rubber spatula to push the puree into a neat mound.
3 Cover tightly with plastic wrap and refrigerate until needed. It will keep for months. To keep indefinitely, cover with a film of olive oil.
